groups.name: Correctness
groups.description: Wrong, unsafe, or structurally invalid code patterns.

groups.name: Anti-pattern
groups.description: Discouraged patterns that often lead to bugs or confusing behavior.

groups.name: Effect-native
groups.description: Prefer Effect-native APIs and abstractions when available.

groups.name: Style
groups.description: Cleanup, consistency, and idiomatic Effect code.

rules.name: anyUnknownInErrorContext
rules.description: Detects 'any' or 'unknown' types in Effect error or requirements channels

diagnostics.text:
This has unknown in the requirements channel and any in the error channel which is not recommended.
Only service identifiers should appear in the requirements channel.
Having an unknown or any error type is not useful. Consider instead using specific error types baked by Data.TaggedError for example.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

This has unknown in the requirements channel which is not recommended.
Only service identifiers should appear in the requirements channel.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

This has any in the error channel which is not recommended.
Having an unknown or any error type is not useful. Consider instead using specific error types baked by Data.TaggedError for example.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

rules.name: classSelfMismatch
rules.description: Ensures Self type parameter matches the class name in Service/Tag/Schema classes

diagnostics.text: Self type parameter should be 'InvalidContextTag'    effect(classSelfMismatch)

rules.name: duplicatePackage
rules.description: Detects when multiple versions of the same Effect package are loaded

rules.name: floatingEffect
rules.description: Ensures Effects are yielded or assigned to variables, not left floating

preview.sourceText:
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

Effect.log("forgotten")

diagnostics.text: Effect must be yielded or assigned to a variable.    effect(floatingEffect)

rules.name: genericEffectServices
rules.description: Prevents services with type parameters that cannot be discriminated at runtime

diagnostics.text: Effect Services with type parameters are not supported because they cannot be properly discriminated at runtime, which may cause unexpected behavior.    effect(genericEffectServices)

rules.name: missingEffectContext
rules.description: Reports missing service requirements in Effect context channel

diagnostics.text: Missing 'Db' in the expected Effect context.    effect(missingEffectContext)

rules.name: missingEffectError
rules.description: Reports missing error types in Effect error channel

diagnostics.text: Missing 'Boom' in the expected Effect errors.    effect(missingEffectError)

rules.name: missingLayerContext
rules.description: Reports missing service requirements in Layer context channel

diagnostics.text: Missing 'A' in the expected Layer context.    effect(missingLayerContext)

rules.name: missingReturnYieldStar
rules.description: Suggests using 'return yield*' for Effects with never success for better type narrowing

diagnostics.text: It is recommended to use return yield* for Effects that never succeed to signal a definitive exit point for type narrowing and tooling support.    effect(missingReturnYieldStar)

rules.name: missingStarInYieldEffectGen
rules.description: Enforces using 'yield*' instead of 'yield' when yielding Effects in generators

diagnostics.text: Seems like you used yield instead of yield* inside this Effect.gen.    effect(missingStarInYieldEffectGen)
When yielding Effects inside Effect.gen, you should use yield* instead of yield.    effect(missingStarInYieldEffectGen)

rules.name: nonObjectEffectServiceType
rules.description: Ensures Effect.Service types are objects, not primitives

diagnostics.text:
Effect.Service requires the service type to be an object {} and not a primitive type. 
Consider wrapping the value in an object, or manually using Context.Tag or Effect.Tag if you want to use a primitive instead.    effect(nonObjectEffectServiceType)

rules.name: outdatedApi
rules.description: Detects usage of APIs that have been removed or renamed in Effect v4

diagnostics.text: This project targets Effect v4, but is using Effect v3 APIs. To find the correct API to use, clone and consult the github.com/effect-ts/effect-smol repository for the corresponding v4 replacement.    effect(outdatedApi)
runtime is an Effect v3 API, but the project is using Effect v4.    effect(outdatedApi)

rules.name: outdatedEffectCodegen
rules.description: Detects when generated code is outdated and needs to be regenerated

diagnostics.text: Codegen accessors result is outdated    effect(outdatedEffectCodegen)

rules.name: overriddenSchemaConstructor
rules.description: Prevents overriding constructors in Schema classes which breaks decoding behavior

diagnostics.text: Classes extending Schema must not override the constructor; this is because it silently breaks the schema decoding behaviour. If that's needed, we recommend instead to use a static 'new' method that constructs the instance.    effect(overriddenSchemaConstructor)

rules.name: unsupportedServiceAccessors
rules.description: Warns about service accessors that need codegen due to generic/complex signatures

diagnostics.text: Even if accessors are enabled, accessors for 'get' won't be available because the signature have generic type parameters or multiple call signatures.    effect(unsupportedServiceAccessors)

rules.name: catchUnfailableEffect
rules.description: Warns when using error handling on Effects that never fail (error type is 'never')

diagnostics.text: Looks like the previous effect never fails, so probably this error handling will never be triggered.    effect(catchUnfailableEffect)

rules.name: effectFnIife
rules.description: Effect.fn or Effect.fnUntraced is called as an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression). Use Effect.gen instead.

diagnostics.text: Effect.fn returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but here it's called immediately. Use Effect.gen instead with Effect.withSpan("preview") piped in the end to mantain tracing spans.    effect(effectFnIife)

rules.name: effectGenUsesAdapter
rules.description: Warns when using the deprecated adapter parameter in Effect.gen

diagnostics.text: The adapter of Effect.gen is not required anymore, it is now just an alias of pipe.    effect(effectGenUsesAdapter)

rules.name: effectInFailure
rules.description: Warns when an Effect is used inside an Effect failure channel

diagnostics.text: The error channel contains an Effect (Effect<void, never, never>). Putting Effect computations in the failure channel is not intended; keep only failure types there.    effect(effectInFailure)
The error channel contains an Effect (Effect<void, never, never>). Putting Effect computations in the failure channel is not intended; keep only failure types there.    effect(effectInFailure)

rules.name: effectInVoidSuccess
rules.description: Detects nested Effects in void success channels that may cause unexecuted effects

diagnostics.text: There is a nested 'Effect<void, never, never>' in the 'void' success channel, beware that this could lead to nested Effect<Effect<...>> that won't be executed.    effect(effectInVoidSuccess)

rules.name: globalErrorInEffectCatch
rules.description: Warns when catch callbacks return global Error type instead of typed errors

diagnostics.text: The 'catch' callback in Effect.tryPromise returns global 'Error', which loses type safety as untagged errors merge together. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property.    effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)

rules.name: globalErrorInEffectFailure
rules.description: Warns when the global Error type is used in an Effect failure channel

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.fail(new Error("boom"))

diagnostics.text: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property.    effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)

rules.name: layerMergeAllWithDependencies
rules.description: Detects interdependencies in Layer.mergeAll calls where one layer provides a service that another layer requires

diagnostics.text: This layer provides A which is required by another layer in the same Layer.mergeAll call. Layer.mergeAll creates layers in parallel, so dependencies between layers will not be satisfied. Consider moving this layer into a Layer.provideMerge after the Layer.mergeAll.    effect(layerMergeAllWithDependencies)

rules.name: leakingRequirements
rules.description: Detects implementation services leaked in service methods

diagnostics.text:
Methods of this Service require `FileSystem` from every caller.

This leaks implementation details into the service's public type — callers shouldn't need to know *how* the service works internally, only *what* it provides.

Resolve these dependencies at Layer creation and provide them to each method, so the service's type reflects its purpose, not its implementation.

To suppress this diagnostic for specific dependency types that are intentionally passed through (e.g., HttpServerRequest), add `@effect-leakable-service` JSDoc to their interface declarations (e.g., the `FileSystem` interface), or to this service by adding a `@effect-expect-leaking FileSystem` JSDoc.

More info and examples at https://effect.website/docs/requirements-management/layers/#avoiding-requirement-leakage    effect(leakingRequirements)

rules.name: multipleEffectProvide
rules.description: Warns against chaining Effect.provide calls which can cause service lifecycle issues

diagnostics.text: Avoid chaining Effect.provide calls, as this can lead to service lifecycle issues. Instead, merge layers and provide them in a single call.    effect(multipleEffectProvide)

rules.name: returnEffectInGen
rules.description: Warns when returning an Effect in a generator causes nested Effect<Effect<...>>

diagnostics.text:
You are returning an Effect-able type inside a generator function, and will result in nested Effect<Effect<...>>.
Maybe you wanted to return yield* instead?
Nested Effect-able types may be intended if you plan to later manually flatten or unwrap this Effect, if so you can safely disable this diagnostic for this line through quickfixes.    effect(returnEffectInGen)

rules.name: runEffectInsideEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Runtime methods instead of Effect.run* inside Effect contexts

diagnostics.text:
Using Effect.runSync inside an Effect is not recommended. The same runtime should generally be used instead to run child effects.
Consider extracting the Runtime by using for example Effect.runtime and then use Runtime.runSync with the extracted runtime instead.    effect(runEffectInsideEffect)

rules.name: schemaSyncInEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Effect-based Schema methods instead of sync methods inside Effect generators

diagnostics.text: Using Schema.decodeSync inside an Effect generator is not recommended. Use Schema.decode instead to get properly typed error channel.    effect(schemaSyncInEffect)

rules.name: scopeInLayerEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Layer.scoped instead of Layer.effect when Scope is in requirements

diagnostics.text:
Seems like you are constructing a layer with a scope in the requirements.
Consider using "scoped" instead to get rid of the scope in the requirements.    effect(scopeInLayerEffect)

rules.name: strictEffectProvide
rules.description: Warns when using Effect.provide with layers outside of application entry points

diagnostics.text: Effect.provide with a Layer should only be used at application entry points. If this is an entry point, you can safely disable this diagnostic. Otherwise, using Effect.provide may break scope lifetimes. Compose all layers at your entry point and provide them at once.    effect(strictEffectProvide)

rules.name: tryCatchInEffectGen
rules.description: Discourages try/catch in Effect generators in favor of Effect error handling

diagnostics.text: Avoid using try/catch inside Effect generators. Use Effect's error handling mechanisms instead (e.g. Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag).    effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)

rules.name: unknownInEffectCatch
rules.description: Warns when catch callbacks return unknown instead of typed errors

diagnostics.text:
The 'catch' callback in Effect.tryPromise returns 'unknown'. The catch callback should be used to provide typed errors.
Consider wrapping unknown errors into Effect's Data.TaggedError for example, or narrow down the type to the specific error raised.    effect(unknownInEffectCatch)

rules.name: extendsNativeError
rules.description: Warns when a class directly extends the native Error class

preview.sourceText: export class PreviewError extends Error {}

diagnostics.text: Avoid extending the native 'Error' class directly. Consider using a tagged error (e.g. Data.TaggedError) to maintain type safety in the Effect failure channel.    effect(extendsNativeError)

rules.name: globalFetch
rules.description: Warns when using the global fetch function instead of the Effect HTTP client

preview.sourceText: export const preview = fetch("https://example.com")

diagnostics.text: Prefer using HttpClient from @effect/platform instead of the global 'fetch' function.    effect(globalFetch)

rules.name: instanceOfSchema
rules.description: Suggests using Schema.is instead of instanceof for Effect Schema types

preview.sourceText:
import { Schema } from "effect"

class User extends Schema.Class<User>("User")({ name: Schema.String }) {}
declare const value: unknown
export const preview = value instanceof User

diagnostics.text: Consider using Schema.is instead of instanceof for Effect Schema types.    effect(instanceOfSchema)

rules.name: nodeBuiltinImport
rules.description: Warns when importing Node.js built-in modules that have Effect-native counterparts

preview.sourceText:
import fs from "node:fs"

export const preview = fs.readFileSync

diagnostics.text: Prefer using FileSystem from @effect/platform instead of the Node.js 'fs' module.    effect(nodeBuiltinImport)

rules.name: preferSchemaOverJson
rules.description: Suggests using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify which may throw

diagnostics.text: Consider using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify    effect(preferSchemaOverJson)

rules.name: catchAllToMapError
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.mapError instead of Effect.catchAll when the callback only wraps the error with Effect.fail

diagnostics.text: You can use Effect.mapError instead of Effect.catch + Effect.fail to transform the error type.    effect(catchAllToMapError)

rules.name: deterministicKeys
rules.description: Enforces deterministic naming for service/tag/error identifiers based on class names

diagnostics.text: Key should be '@effect/harness-effect-v4/examples/diagnostics/deterministicKeys_preview/RenamedService'    effect(deterministicKeys)

rules.name: effectFnOpportunity
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.fn for functions that returns an Effect

diagnostics.text: Can be rewritten as a reusable function: Effect.fn(function*() { ... })    effect(effectFnOpportunity)

rules.name: effectMapVoid
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.asVoid instead of Effect.map(() => void 0), Effect.map(() => undefined), or Effect.map(() => {})

diagnostics.text: Effect.asVoid can be used instead to discard the success value    effect(effectMapVoid)

rules.name: effectSucceedWithVoid
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.void instead of Effect.succeed(undefined) or Effect.succeed(void 0)

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.succeed(undefined)

diagnostics.text: Effect.void can be used instead of Effect.succeed(undefined) or Effect.succeed(void 0)    effect(effectSucceedWithVoid)

rules.name: importFromBarrel
rules.description: Suggests importing from specific module paths instead of barrel exports

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.void

diagnostics.text: Importing from barrel module effect is not allowed.    effect(importFromBarrel)

rules.name: missedPipeableOpportunity
rules.description: Enforces the use of pipeable style for nested function calls

diagnostics.text: Nested function calls can be converted to pipeable style for better readability; consider using Schema.decodeEffect(User)({ id: 1 }).pipe(...) instead.    effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)

rules.name: missingEffectServiceDependency
rules.description: Checks that Effect.Service dependencies satisfy all required layer inputs

diagnostics.text: Service 'Db' is required but not provided by dependencies    effect(missingEffectServiceDependency)

rules.name: redundantSchemaTagIdentifier
rules.description: Suggests removing redundant identifier argument when it equals the tag value in Schema.TaggedClass/TaggedError/TaggedRequest

diagnostics.text: Identifier 'Preview' is redundant since it equals the _tag value    effect(redundantSchemaTagIdentifier)

rules.name: schemaStructWithTag
rules.description: Suggests using Schema.TaggedStruct instead of Schema.Struct with _tag field

diagnostics.text: Schema.Struct with a _tag field can be simplified to Schema.TaggedStruct to make the tag optional in the constructor.    effect(schemaStructWithTag)

rules.name: schemaUnionOfLiterals
rules.description: Simplifies Schema.Union of multiple Schema.Literal calls into single Schema.Literal

preview.sourceText:
import * as Schema from "effect/Schema"

export const preview = Schema.Union(Schema.Literal("a"), Schema.Literal("b"))

diagnostics.text: A Schema.Union of multiple Schema.Literal calls can be simplified to a single Schema.Literal call.    effect(schemaUnionOfLiterals)

rules.name: serviceNotAsClass
rules.description: Warns when ServiceMap.Service is used as a variable instead of a class declaration

preview.sourceText:
import { ServiceMap } from "effect"

export const Preview = ServiceMap.Service<{ port: number }>("Preview")

diagnostics.text: ServiceMap.Service should be used in a class declaration instead of as a variable. Use: class Preview extends ServiceMap.Service<Preview, { port: number }>()("Preview") {}    effect(serviceNotAsClass)

rules.name: strictBooleanExpressions
rules.description: Enforces boolean types in conditional expressions for type safety

preview.sourceText:
declare const value: string | undefined
export const preview = value ? 1 : 0

diagnostics.text: Unexpected `string` type in condition, expected strictly a boolean instead.    effect(strictBooleanExpressions)
Unexpected `undefined` type in condition, expected strictly a boolean instead.    effect(strictBooleanExpressions)

rules.name: unnecessaryEffectGen
rules.description: Suggests removing Effect.gen when it contains only a single return statement

diagnostics.text: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement.    effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)

rules.name: unnecessaryFailYieldableError
rules.description: Suggests yielding yieldable errors directly instead of wrapping with Effect.fail

diagnostics.text: This Effect.fail call uses a yieldable error type as argument. You can yield* the error directly instead.    effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)

rules.name: unnecessaryPipe
rules.description: Removes pipe calls with no arguments

preview.sourceText:
import { pipe } from "effect/Function"

export const preview = pipe(1)

diagnostics.text: This pipe call contains no arguments.    effect(unnecessaryPipe)

rules.name: unnecessaryPipeChain
rules.description: Simplifies chained pipe calls into a single pipe call

preview.sourceText:
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

export const preview = Effect.succeed(1).pipe(Effect.asVoid).pipe(Effect.as("done"))

diagnostics.text: Chained pipe calls can be simplified to a single pipe call    effect(unnecessaryPipeChain)
